ogTitle: Toms Creek vs Tonderburine: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Toms Creek and Tonderburine.
heading: Toms Creek vs Tonderburine — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Toms Creek, New South Wales and Tonderburine, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Tonderburine has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Toms Creek (45/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Toms Creek or Tonderburine safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Toms Creek has a flood risk score of 46/100, while Tonderburine scores 58/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Toms Creek scores 39/100 for bushfire risk. Tonderburine scores 17/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Toms Creek or Tonderburine?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Tonderburine has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.17% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
